$schema_comment: Release candidate manifest. Machine-readable declaration of what this release includes, claims, and requires.

required_test_commands.name: Web client tests
required_test_commands.command: cd client && npm ci && npm test

required_test_commands.name: Plugin tests
required_test_commands.command: cd plugin && npm ci && npm test

required_test_commands.name: Plugin type check
required_test_commands.command: cd plugin && npm ci && npm run typecheck

required_test_commands.name: Plugin lifecycle smoke test (local OpenClaw runtime)

security_claims.key_model: Static X25519 identity keypairs are reused for each long-lived identity. Gateway/plugin and SDK deployments can persist them across restarts. The web reference client persists its keypair in IndexedDB when available, so the same browser identity survives reconnects and normal page reloads in the same browser profile. If IndexedDB is unavailable or persistence fails, it falls back to a page-memory identity that is regenerated after reload.
security_claims.notes: v1 does NOT provide forward secrecy. Compromising an identity private key allows computing the ECDH shared secret and (with captured session nonces) deriving all past and future session keys. ADR needed for v2 (consider Noise IK/XX).

known_limitations: Single relay node only — no clustering, federation, or HA
Stream resumption not supported — interrupted streams require new request
Requests are not idempotent by default — no automatic retries for state-changing methods
No CA/PKI-backed gateway identity. The browser reference client uses a user-supplied pinned gateway public key and production deployments should verify that key out of band.
JavaScript SDK is not yet implemented
OpenClaw gateway plugin requires a current OpenClaw runtime with channel and CLI plugin APIs enabled
Browser identity persistence depends on IndexedDB availability; blocked environments fall back to a page-memory identity after reload
